news.headline: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 6 April: Strikes persist across region amid rising humanitarian needs
news.summary: Strikes and counter-strikes continue across the Middle East, with dozens of casualties reported over the weekend in Lebanon following Israeli strikes targeting the south and the capital, Beirut. As hostilities persist into their second month, humanitarian needs are rising, critical infrastructure re

news.headline: Has Trump confirmed Iran’s claim that protesters were US-armed?
news.summary: US president says Washington armed Kurdish Iranian opposition groups during the January demonstrations.

news.headline: Iran threatens Bab al-Mandeb closure: How would that affect world trade?
news.summary: If the chokepoint were shut, along with the Strait of Hormuz, a quarter of the world's energy supply would be blocked.

news.headline: Pakistan offers ‘two-phased’ truce deal to end US-Israel war on Iran
news.summary: Iran, while still reviewing the proposal, says it won't reopen Hormuz as part of a temporary ceasefire.

news.headline: Trump’s ‘Bridge Day’ threat: Can a last-ditch ceasefire plan work?
news.summary: Mediators Pakistan, Turkiye and Egypt are proposing a 45-day ceasefire plan. But Iran is reluctant.

news.headline: Real Madrid vs Bayern Munich: Champions League – team news, start, lineups
news.summary: Madrid host Bundesliga champions Bayern Munich for the first leg of their blockbuster Champions League quarterfinal tie.

news.headline: Israel destroys southern Lebanon towns, hits ‘safe’ areas around Beirut
news.summary: The Israeli military launches new attacks on Beirut, southern Lebanon as humanitarian crisis deepens.

news.headline: World Cup qualification earns Iraq coach Arnold a hero’s welcome in Sydney
news.summary: Graham Arnold was received by jubilant Iraqi fans upon his return to Australia after a successful qualification campaign.

news.headline: Up to 1.2 million people forced to flee as Israel pummels Lebanon
news.summary: Families face repeated trauma as Israeli attacks displace nearly 1.2 million, many for the second time in recent months.

news.headline: Iraqis give rousing welcome to coach behind historic World Cup return
news.summary: Iraqis in Sydney turned out in full force to welcome coach Graham Arnold as he returned to Australia.

news.headline: Why did ‘Handala’ target FBI Director Kash Patel?
news.summary: Al Jazeera’s Linh Nguyen explains the Handala Hack Team.

news.headline: Families fleeing Israeli attacks take refuge in Lebanon’s mountains
news.summary: Over one million people displaced across Lebanon as Israel escalates military campaign with air raids, ground invasion.

news.headline: Yemen’s civilians fear fallout after Houthis enter Iran war
news.summary: Fears of air strikes and rising prices dominate everyday life in Sanaa as Yemen becomes embroiled in regional conflict.

news.headline: Trump says US armed Iranian dissidents via Kurds, Kurdish groups deny claim
news.summary: Trump's admission gives credence to Iranian assertion that the January protests were backed by the West to create chaos.

news.headline: A visual guide to Artemis II and previous missions to the moon
news.summary: Artemis II is expected to break the record for the farthest distance humans have travelled from Earth.

news.headline: Iran’s top university bombed as US, Israel intensify attacks; 34 killed
news.summary: Tehran says it will respond 'in kind' to attacks on its infrastructure, accuses Trump of inciting 'war crimes'.

news.headline: Iran war: What is happening on day 38 of US-Israeli attacks?
news.summary: Tensions continue after Trump's threat on Truth Social for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

news.headline: ‘Cocktail of Hindutva and welfarism’: How Modi’s BJP is wooing Assam voters
news.summary: Party banks on mix of xenophobic anti-Muslim policies and financial aid schemes aimed at women to retain power in state.

news.headline: Man City captain Silva to leave Premier League club at season’s end
news.summary: Silva to leave Manchester City after winning the Champions League and six Premier League titles in his nine-year stint.

news.headline: Steph Curry returns from injury with 29 points but Warriors lose to Rockets
news.summary: NBA star marks comeback after nine weeks as Houston Rockets beat Golden State Warriors 117-116 in San Francisco.

news.headline: A new regional order for the Strait of Hormuz
news.summary: The GCC and Iran can come up with a new security architecture for the Strait of Hormuz that can give Trump an off-ramp.

news.headline: UN nuclear agency chief ‘deeply concerned’ by reports of latest attack on Iran power plant
news.summary: Reports of yet another projectile strike near the Bushehr nuclear power plant prompted Rafael Grossi, head of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA), to register his deep concern on Saturday.

news.headline: ‘We watched them die before our eyes’: Sudan health workers helpless amid medical shortages
news.summary: As violence forces tens of thousands to flee Sudan’s South Kordofan state, doctors in a key maternity hospital are facing impossible choices – with too few supplies, too many patients, and lives slipping away.

news.headline: Deminers race to keep up with military technology
news.summary: In conflict zones where new technologies are making landmines more dangerous, deminers must innovate at the same pace to avoid being left behind, a leading UN mines expert has told UN News.&nbsp;

news.headline: World News in Brief: South Sudan rights, opioid guidelines update, DR Congo crisis continues
news.summary: South Sudan is evolving into a catastrophic human rights and humanitarian crisis, UN Human Rights Council-appointed independent experts warned on Thursday.

news.headline: Guterres warns of ‘wider war’ as Middle East conflict enters second month
news.summary: The Middle East crisis has lurched into its second month, prompting UN Secretary-General António Guterres to issue a stark warning on Thursday morning that the world is “on the edge of a wider war” with catastrophic global implications.

news.headline: Middle East crisis exposes global energy fault line as UN urges shift to renewables
news.summary: The ongoing crisis in the Middle East is exposing a central vulnerability in the global economy: the dependence on fossil fuels flowing through regions affected by conflict, a situation which is strengthening the UN’s case for a faster transition to cheaper, more resilient renewable power.

news.headline: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 2 April: Civilian impact deepens as UN chief says ‘we must find a peaceful way out’ before region is engulfed by war
news.summary: Deadly new strikes reported across the Middle East overnight and Thursday rattled energy markets and pushed crude oil prices up to $107 in early trading. Hopes dwindled of a quick end to the conflict as President Trump spoke of another "two to three weeks" of attacks, alongside "ongoi

news.headline: World News in Brief: Lebanon peacekeeper deaths, Haiti mission support, drought and conflict in Somalia
news.summary: Although the situation on the ground is challenging, investigations are underway into the killing of three UN peacekeepers over two consecutive days with an update expected “as soon as possible,” UN Spokesperson Stéphane Dujarric said on Wednesday.&nbsp;

news.headline: Middle East war: Energy crunch hits vulnerable nations
news.summary: The war in the Middle East and the near halt to shipping in the&nbsp;Strait of Hormuz has amplified the energy crunch facing developing nations in Africa and&nbsp;South Asia that rely heavily on imported liquid gas, food and fertilizers.&nbsp;

news.headline: Breaking the Gaza aid bottleneck: 106-tonne delivery arrives via new sea route
news.summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) has facilitated the delivery of some 106 metric tonnes of lifesaving nutrition supplies to the Gaza Strip – the first shipment via a mechanism to deliver aid by sea, in line with a UN Security Council resolution and amid the ongoing war in the Middle East.&nbs

news.headline: Middle East war: UN’s Türk decries ‘severe restrictions’ on free speech
news.summary: From Iran to multiple nations in the Gulf and the wider Middle East, around 3,000 people have reportedly been arrested in the first month of the ongoing war across the region, sparking alarm over free speech restrictions and state repression.

news.headline: Lebanon at ‘breaking point’ as displacement soars and strikes intensify
news.summary: The UN’s top humanitarian official warned the Security Council on Tuesday that Lebanon is facing one of its most dangerous moments in years, with escalating violence, mass displacement and deepening human suffering pushing the country to “breaking point”.

news.headline: Lebanon: The UN peacekeepers helping communities who’ve stayed behind
news.summary: The killing of three UN peacekeepers in south Lebanon in the past two days has highlighted the grave dangers for the 10,000 military personnel who make up the bulk of UNIFIL, the UN Interim Force in Lebanon – and for the communities caught up in intense clashes between the Israeli military and Hezbo

news.headline: Syria: Hundreds of thousands flee Lebanon, vital food aid blocked
news.summary: The trauma of mass displacement and disruption to aid shipments throughout the world are among the devastating impacts of the war raging in the Middle East, UN agencies said on Tuesday.

news.headline: ‘No precedent’ for seafarers caught in war zone in post-WW2 era
news.summary: Some 20,000 seafarers remain stranded on ships in the Strait of Hormuz as the war in the Middle East continues, a situation which has been described as unprecedented in the post-Second World War era.

news.headline: MIDDLE EAST LIVE: Vital food aid blocked, Security Council meets in emergency session on Lebanon
news.summary: More than a month since war erupted in the Middle East, UN agencies confirmed on Tuesday that huge numbers of people have returned to Syria from Lebanon "exhausted, traumatized and with very, very few belongings". Meanwhile, the UN International Maritime Organization said that another vess

news.headline: Airstrike on funeral underscores rising civilian toll in Sudan
news.summary: Fears are mounting for civilians caught up in Sudan’s deadly war between rival militaries as attacks intensify and humanitarian access shrinks, following a deadly airstrike on a funeral gathering in West Kordofan.

news.headline: The image from Gaza that still haunts me: Palestine relief agency chief
news.summary: Asking the softly spoken, veteran humanitarian worker Philippe Lazzarini how he feels as he comes to the end of his second term as the head of the UN agency for Palestinians, UNRWA, is perhaps an unfair question.&nbsp;

news.headline: UN condemns killing of two more peacekeepers in Lebanon
news.summary: The United Nations has condemned two consecutive days of deadly attacks on peacekeepers serving with the UN Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL), amid rising hostilities between Israeli forces and Hezbollah militants.&nbsp;

news.headline: World News in Brief: Ukraine drone attacks, Afghan rights, Zero-Waste Day
news.summary: A night of drone attacks reportedly killed two people and injured 12 in the Ukrainian port city of Odesa, as a maternity hospital and three educational facilities were also damaged.

news.headline: Sea mines clearance: A new dimension of difficulty
news.summary: Clearing mines laid at sea is an “extremely challenging and very dangerous” mission, with tidal currents just one of myriad other risk factors to consider before attempting to make a stretch of water safe, a leading UN mines expert tells us.

news.headline: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 30 March: UN peacekeepers killed amid Israel-Hezbollah clashes
news.summary: Further attacks have been reported across the Middle East as the war enters a second month, with two more Indonesian peacekeepers killed in Southern Lebanon on Monday, following the death of a fellow ‘blue helmet’ a day earlier. On the diplomatic front, the UN has announced a taskforce to restore th

news.headline: UN peacekeeper killed, another seriously injured, in southern Lebanon
news.summary: UN Secretary-General António Guterres called for accountability after a peacekeeper was killed in Lebanon on Sunday amid hostilities between Israel and Hezbollah.

news.headline: Middle East war: UN initiatives support mediation efforts, ‘lifesaving’ fertiliser shipments
news.summary: Just hours after war broke out in the Middle East last month, United Nations Secretary-General António Guterres warned the Security Council that the fighting risked “igniting a chain of events that no one can control in the most volatile region of the world.”

news.headline: Kidnapped and tortured on an infamous migration route to Saudi Arabia
news.summary: An Ethiopian man describes how he was tortured by human traffickers as he went in search of his nephew on a now infamous migration route from the Horn of Africa through Yemen to Saudi Arabia.

news.headline: Syria: UN Commission documents grave violations in July 2025 escalation in Sweida
news.summary: More than 1,700 people were killed and nearly 200,000 more displaced in the July 2025 massacres in Sweida, Syria, UN human rights investigators said on Friday in a report&nbsp;that called for greater action towards accountability.&nbsp;

news.headline: Middle East war: Attacks on vital healthcare, evacuation strike fears
news.summary: Almost one month since Israeli and US airstrikes on Iran began, sparking a wider regional war, UN agencies and partners on Friday highlighted the terror among civilians fleeing bombardment, with “no safe space” to go.

news.headline: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 27 March: Humanitarian crisis deepens across the region
news.summary: The war in the Middle East continues, with attacks causing further terror and suffering, deepening the humanitarian crisis across the region. The UN announced the launch of a task force to meet the immense needs. &nbsp;In Geneva, diplomats at the Human Rights Council have been discussing the sch

news.headline: Myanmar: WFP reports fragile recovery one year after deadly earthquake
news.summary: A “new wave of global instability is hitting Myanmar at the worst possible moment,” a UN official in the country warned on Friday, as increases in fuel, food, and fertilizer prices due to the ongoing Middle East conflict push vulnerable families closer to hunger one year after a devastating earthqua

news.headline: Ukraine: danger is only increasing, warns UN human rights office
news.summary: More than four years since Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine, the “danger is only increasing”, particularly from attack drones, a top UN human rights official warned on Thursday.

news.headline: Sudan: Hospital strike highlights surge in drone attacks on civilians
news.summary: The death toll from a horrific attack on a hospital in Sudan’s Darfur has risen further, amid a “sharp increase” in drone attacks against civilians this year, UN agencies said on Tuesday.

news.headline: Haitian gangs expand reach as police are accused of ‘summary executions’
news.summary: Deadly gangs in Haiti are expanding their reach to include control over key sea and road routes as police in the beleaguered Caribbean island nation are being accused of using “unnecessary and disproportionate lethal force and summary executions.”

news.headline: Ukraine violence ‘worse than ever’, Security Council hears
news.summary: More than four years after Russia launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine, “the violence is worse than ever” and far from abating, the head of UN political and peacebuilding affairs said on Monday in a briefing to the Security Council.&nbsp;

news.headline: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Ukraine in spotlight as Russian strikes surge
news.summary: The Security Council is met for an open briefing on Ukraine, requested by European members following Kyiv’s 18 March letter citing a surge in Russian strikes. Inside the country, attacks continue to take a heavy toll: since 19 March, at least 25 civilians have reportedly been killed and more than 13

news.headline: How Kyrgyzstan built a system to support domestic violence survivors
news.summary: Domestic violence was not something people spoke about openly in Kyrgyzstan in the 1990s following the collapse of the Soviet Union, but after a long road of dedicated efforts, there are now laws addressing family abuse, crisis centres and hotlines while human rights defenders tackle such new challe

news.headline: WHO verifies deadly hospital attack in war-torn Sudan
news.summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) verified on Saturday a hospital attack in war-torn Sudan that killed 64 people.

news.headline: Forests are more than a resource: How Belarus protects its vital ecosystem
news.summary: Forests cover more than 40 per cent of Belarus. At the same time, the country recorded twice as many forest fires last year compared to the year before.

news.headline: The UN housing development which challenged 1940s’ segregated United States
news.summary: At a time when some state laws dictated where different races could live, Parkway Village, built to house some of the first UN staff in New York in 1947, led the way in eliminating racially segregated housing in the United States.

news.headline: Middle East war shockwaves ripple through Asia-Pacific fuel and supply chains
news.summary: The fallout from the war in the Middle East is rippling far beyond the Gulf, disrupting fuel supplies, shipping routes and supply chains across Asia and the Pacific, with some of the region’s most vulnerable economies already feeling the strain through rising prices, rationing and threats to jobs, f

news.headline: Afghanistan: UN condemns deadly attack on rehab centre in Kabul
news.summary: Top UN officials condemned on Tuesday Pakistan’s overnight strike on a rehab centre that reportedly killed at least 400 people in Kabul, according to Taliban authorities, and injured more than 250 others.

news.headline: Deepfakes, voice cloning and weaponised AI: Global wake-up call to organised fraud
news.summary: The Sawyers from Australia were never really interested in volatile investing. As their retirement age approached, the idea of a low-risk investment for their pension seemed attractive. But one day, after clicking on a seemingly legitimate online advert that offered a reasonable risk-averse plan, th

news.headline: ‘Hope’ for Haiti’s political future as some 300 groups reportedly register for upcoming elections
news.summary: Haiti remains mired in a multidimensional crisis marked by weak institutions, political uncertainty, widespread gang violence and overwhelming humanitarian needs, but a recent new agreement by political groups offers “a moment of hope and progress for the Haitian people”, according to the UN’s most

news.headline: ‘Glimmer of hope’ in Haiti amid shifting gang frontlines
news.summary: The liberation of territory from gangs and a more “motivated and visible” police presence has provided a “glimmer of hope” for Haiti as the Caribbean island nation continues to struggle with violence, insecurity and poverty.

news.headline: Myanmar at a ‘crossroads’: The world must not forsake civilians there, urges UN expert
news.summary: More than five years after Myanmar’s military coup, international resolve to hold the junta accountable must not weaken, an independent human rights expert warned on Friday, as escalating violence and growing humanitarian needs push millions of civilians deeper into crisis.

news.headline: Russia deportation of Ukraine children is crime against humanity: independent probe
news.summary: Scores of Ukrainian children are still missing after being deported far and wide across Russia and occupied territories while their families continue to search for them, human rights investigators said on Thursday.

news.headline: Venezuela’s repressive State apparatus ‘intact’ despite leadership change
news.summary: Venezuela’s repressive State apparatus remains operational despite the seizure of former President Nicolás Maduro by United States forces on 3 January, according to independent UN human rights investigators.

news.headline: UN relief chief condemns '$1 billion-a-day' cost of war in Middle East
news.summary: The UN’s emergency relief chief on Wednesday condemned the “$1 billion-a-day” cost of the war in the Middle East,&nbsp;at a time when humanitarian needs are soaring and aid funding is falling dangerously short.

news.headline: Nicaragua: Rights experts uncover State corruption fuelling repression, spying on exiles
news.summary: Nicaragua’s Government is financing the repression of its opponents through illegal misuse of public funds and targeting exiles through a transnational surveillance and intelligence network, the UN Group of Independent Human Rights Experts on Nicaragua warned in its latest report on Tuesday.

news.headline: Humanitarian crisis ‘worsening’ in Afghanistan as war erupts in the Middle East
news.summary: Crises in the region on both Afghanistan’s longest borders are undermining the country’s stability, a senior UN official warned the Security Council on Monday as concerns over Middle East crisis grow amid clashes with Pakistan and a worsening humanitarian crisis.

news.headline: Radio Begum: A rare public space for women in Afghanistan
news.summary: Every morning in Kabul, several cars make their way across the Afghan capital to pick up the producers of Radio Begum. The young women do not travel to the office on their own as moving around the city has become too complicated.

news.headline: Justice on the move: Mobile courts offer hope for communities in South Sudan
news.summary: Travelling more than 200 kilometres (124 miles) from Yambio, the capital of&nbsp;Western Equatoria State in southwestern South Sudan, a team of justice experts escorted by United Nations peacekeepers moved slowly along rough, dusty roads, determined to reach communities that have waited years fo

news.headline: ‘AI Kid of India’ urges young people to embrace technology
news.summary: 16-year-old Raul John Aju – dubbed the “AI Kid of India” at home – is a business prodigy who advises government and industry and has created several innovative AI tools.&nbsp;

news.headline: Forgotten conflict in South Sudan at ‘a dangerous point’, Türk warns
news.summary: South Sudan was the focus of debate in the UN Human Rights Council on Friday as escalating violence and political tensions – alongside a massive humanitarian emergency and war in neighbouring Sudan – threaten efforts to achieve lasting peace.&nbsp;

news.headline: Eight years of captivity: Finding freedom and healing in Ukraine
news.summary: Four years after Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine, many people are trying to overcome deep-rooted trauma and rebuild what has been lost.&nbsp;

news.headline: Human Rights Council hears of ongoing risk of further genocidal violence in Sudan
news.summary: Nearly three years of war in Sudan have been marked by killings, rape and other violations, with risk of genocidal violence spreading, the UN Human Rights Council heard on Thursday.&nbsp;

news.headline: ‘The trauma will stay with me for life,’ says survivor of sexual violence in war-torn Ukraine
news.summary: One evening in war-ravaged southern Ukraine, a group of armed men entered Maryna’s home. She had nowhere to hide.

news.headline: Humanitarian pressures grow as Cuba continues to struggle with energy shortages
news.summary: Cuba’s humanitarian situation is worsening as fuel shortages deepen nearly a month after Washington took measures to block oil supplies from entering the Caribbean nation, a senior UN official warned on Wednesday.

news.headline: South Sudan: Funding gaps threaten nearly 2 million displaced people
news.summary: Funding shortfalls are putting the lives of more than 1.9 million displaced people in South Sudan at risk amid rising humanitarian needs, the International Organization for Migration (IOM) said on Wednesday.&nbsp;

news.headline: Central African Republic enters ‘decisive’ phase after landmark elections
news.summary: Fresh from holding the most extensive elections in its history, the Central African Republic (CAR) is entering what the UN’s top envoy in the country describes as a “decisive period” in consolidating fragile peace gains, even as security challenges persist and disarmament efforts continue.

news.headline: Concerns raised by Burundian refugees about ‘coercive’ repatriation from Tanzania
news.summary: Concerns have been raised about the “coercive” repatriation from Tanzania of Burundian refugees, many of whom do not want to return to their home country.

news.headline: Somalia: Number of people going hungry nearly doubles in a year
news.summary: The number of people in Somalia facing acute food insecurity has nearly doubled to 6.5 million since last year as hunger levels rise due to worsening drought, conflict and soaring food prices.&nbsp;

news.headline: Ukraine: ‘Use every diplomatic tool to end this war’, top UN official tells Security Council
news.summary: The international community must “use every diplomatic tool” to bring an end to the war in Ukraine, a senior UN official told the Security Council on Tuesday as Russia’s full-scale invasion entered a fifth year.

news.headline: LIVE: UN marks fourth anniversary of Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine
news.summary: Four years after Russia launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine, the UN is marking the day with high-level debate and renewed calls to end the war - including in the General Assembly which passed a resolution reaffirming its strong commitment to the sovereignty, independence and territorial inte

news.headline: Ukraine wakes to more violence as Russia’s full-scale invasion enters fifth year
news.summary: The full-scale invasion of Ukraine by Russian troops on 24 February 2022 shattered the peaceful aspirations of an entire continent, but war must never be the new normal, UN General Assembly President Annalena Baerbock said on Tuesday.

news.headline: South Sudan fighting displaces nearly 280,000; UN warns of ‘perfect storm’
news.summary: Renewed fighting in South Sudan’s Jonglei state has displaced hundreds of thousands of civilians in recent weeks, damaged health facilities, fuelled the spread of cholera and prompting the UN relief chief to warn of a “perfect storm” of conflict, climate shocks and deprivation.

news.headline: Myriad fragments, one tragedy: How four years of war changed Ukraine
news.summary: Although fighting had been raging in the east of Ukraine since Russia's invasion of Crimea in 2014, most in the country did not believe all-out war would occur. With the full-scale invasion now reaching the four-year mark, many Ukrainians cannot believe it has gone on for so long, with no end i

news.headline: Ukraine: $588 billion recovery cost over the next 10 years
news.summary: Reconstruction and recovery in Ukraine will cost almost $588 billion over the next decade, or nearly three times the estimated GDP in 2025, as housing, energy and other critical sectors continue to come under fire from Russian forces.&nbsp;

news.headline: ICC judges assess case against Duterte over Philippines ‘war on drugs’ killings
news.summary: Judges at the UN-backed International Criminal Court (ICC) on Monday opened a confirmation of charges hearing for former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte, to determine whether there is sufficient evidence to send him to trial on alleged crimes against humanity linked to killings during the count

news.headline: Guns, fashionable clothes and death threats: How gangs in Haiti ensnare children
news.summary: A 16-year-old Haitian boy has been talking about how he was lured into working for a criminal gang but then threatened with death when he said he would not fight against the police.

news.headline: Ukraine’s women at breaking point after four years of war as attacks on energy, healthcare continue – UN humanitarians
news.summary: Four years into Russia’s full-scale invasion, millions in Ukraine struggle to keep the lights on and heat their homes, with the crisis taking a particular toll on women, humanitarians warned on Friday.

news.headline: UN report exposes torture, rape in Southeast Asia’s multi-billion-dollar scam centres
news.summary: A sprawling online scam industry worth an estimated tens of billions of dollars a year is being powered by trafficked workers subjected to torture, sexual abuse and forced labour inside heavily guarded compounds in Southeast Asia, a new UN human rights report has found.

news.headline: ‘Alarming increase’ in recruitment of children into gangs in Haiti
news.summary: There has been an “alarming increase” in the number of children being recruited into gangs in Haiti with “devastating consequences” for children, families and society as a whole, the UN reported on Friday.

news.headline: ‘No corner of Sudan is safe’: UN officials warn of famine and atrocities as war intensifies
news.summary: Nearly three years into Sudan’s war, violence is intensifying, famine conditions are looming in parts of the country, and civilians remain trapped between shifting front lines, senior UN political and humanitarian officials told the Security Council on Thursday, warning that the risk of further mass

news.headline: Security Council LIVE: Sudan in focus amid genocide warnings in Darfur
news.summary: The UN Security Council meets this morning to discuss Sudan as the war nears its third year, with fighting intensifying across multiple regions and civilians facing deepening humanitarian catastrophe. Senior UN political and humanitarian officials are expected to brief ministers on a conflict marked

news.headline: Sudan: ‘Hallmarks of genocide’ found in El Fasher, UN investigators detail mass killings and ethnic targeting
news.summary: The Rapid Support Forces (RSF) carried out ethnically targeted killings, widespread sexual violence and enforced disappearances during their late-October takeover of El Fasher in Sudan’s Darfur region — acts that a UN fact-finding mission said show “hallmarks of genocide” against the Zaghawa and Fur

news.headline: UNICEF calls for release of children detained during Iran protests
news.summary: The UN Children’s Fund (UNICEF) has called for the immediate release of all children detained during the recent public unrest in Iran, expressing deep concern that minors arrested in connection with the protests remain behind bars.

news.headline: Sudan: Rights chief alarmed after at least 57 killed in drone attacks in two days
news.summary: The UN High Commissioner for Human Rights called on Wednesday for warring parties in Sudan to take urgent steps to protect civilians after at least 57 people were killed in separate drone strikes over two days this week.&nbsp;

news.headline: UN Libya envoy warns Security Council political deadlock threatens state unity
news.summary: Libya’s prolonged political stalemate, worsening economic conditions and an emerging split within the judiciary pose mounting risks to the country’s unity, the UN’s top envoy for the country, Hanna Serwaa Tetteh, told the Security Council on Wednesday.

news.headline: Decades of gains overshadowed as Asia-Pacific falls behind on sustainable development targets
news.summary: Climate inaction, biodiversity loss and rising emissions are pushing Asia and the Pacific further off course on the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), with the region set to miss nearly nine out of ten targets by 2030, the United Nations has warned.
